finding_summary: 'Twelve principal pantheon members' is a corpus-invented Theros pantheon tier that does not match canonical structure. Per MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros (verified via WebSearch) the canonical structure is **5 major + 10 minor = 15 gods total**: the 5 mono-color majors (Heliod, Thassa, Erebos, Purphoros, Nylea) plus 10 two-color minors (Athreos, Ephara, Iroas, Karametra, Keranos, Kruphix, Mogis, Pharika, Phenax, Xenagos). The corpus has invented a 'twelve principal' tier asserting 5 majors + 7 selected minors (Iroas, Karametra, Mogis, Pharika, Phenax, Kruphix, Athreos) are 'principal' while the other 3 minors (Xenagos, Ephara, Keranos) are 'minor-tier additions'. This split has no canonical basis. The framing recurs in: iroas.md (lines 31, 37, 72), mogis.md (lines 35, 41, 80), heliod.md (lines 34, 40), erebos.md (line 35), nylea.md (lines 35, 41, 82), and theros-pantheon.md (lines 12, 98, 162). The theros-pantheon.md line 98 is the root: 'Twelve principal gods (Heliod, Erebos, Thassa, Purphoros, Nylea, Pharika, Iroas, Karametra, Mogis, Phenax, Kruphix, Athreos) plus three minor-tier additions (Xenagos... Ephara, Keranos...)' — and all sub-nodes inherited the fabrication. The actual canonical tier-split is 5/10, with all twelve named gods being canonically minor except for the 5 majors.

fixes.before: faction: "the Pantheon of Theros — the ruling body of fifteen gods (twelve principal Nyx-born deities plus three demigod / minor / outsider entries depending on canonical scope) who govern the mortal plane of Theros from the constellation-realm of Nyx; sustained in existence by mortal devotion via the Devotion mechanic that gave the plane its design-language signature"
fixes.after: faction: "the Pantheon of Theros — the ruling body of fifteen gods (the five mono-color principal deities — Heliod, Thassa, Erebos, Purphoros, Nylea — plus the ten two-color minor deities corresponding to the ten color-pair combinations) who govern the mortal plane of Theros from the constellation-realm of Nyx; sustained in existence by mortal devotion via the Devotion mechanic that gave the plane its design-language signature"
fixes.rationale: Correct the line-12 frontmatter faction-string. Replace the invented 'twelve principal + three minor-tier' tiering with the canonical 5-major + 10-minor structure per MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros.

fixes.before: - **The fifteen-god roster is structurally specific.** Twelve principal gods (Heliod, Erebos, Thassa, Purphoros, Nylea, Pharika, Iroas, Karametra, Mogis, Phenax, Kruphix, Athreos) plus three minor-tier additions (Xenagos as the ascended-mortal demigod-then-god from JOU; Ephara, Keranos as the Born of the Gods minor pantheon). The roster is bounded by Wizards's own publication record across THS / BNG / JOU / THB / Theros Stargazing Secret Lair series.
fixes.after: - **The fifteen-god roster is structurally specific.** Per MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros, the canonical tier-split is **five mono-color principal gods** (Heliod-white, Thassa-blue, Erebos-black, Purphoros-red, Nylea-green) plus **ten two-color minor gods** corresponding to the ten color-pair combinations (Athreos W/B, Ephara W/U, Iroas W/R, Karametra W/G, Keranos U/R, Kruphix U/G, Mogis B/R, Pharika B/G, Phenax U/B, Xenagos R/G). The minor gods debuted across Born of the Gods (2014, ten of them) and Journey into Nyx (2014, with Xenagos as the ascended-mortal demigod-then-god capstone). The roster is bounded by Wizards's own publication record across THS / BNG / JOU / THB / Theros Stargazing Secret Lair series.
fixes.rationale: Correct the line-98 Caveats roster claim. Replace the invented 12+3 tier-split with the canonical 5+10 structure and enumerate all ten minor gods with their color-pair attributions.

fixes.before: - **Parent-cohort node, not solo-god nodes.** This entry anchors the pantheon as a single character-cohort node, sibling to [[orzhov-syndicate]] / [[azorius-senate]] / [[phyrexia]] / [[aether-foundation]] / [[valgavoth]] / [[izzet-league]] / [[alola-elite-four]]. When an individual god reaches ≥3 corpus depictions across distinct cards, that god gets its own sibling solo character node and this parent node's `appears_on:` for those god-specific cards is mirrored (not delegated — parent retains the appears_on entries; the sub-node duplicates them, per Mogis / Nylea / Iroas precedent). The parent stays as the unifying-faction anchor; solo gods become subsidiaries that inherit institutional context. Current solo sub-nodes commissioned: [[mogis]] (6 corpus anchors), [[nylea]] (6 corpus anchors), [[iroas]] (3 corpus anchors), [[erebos]] (4 corpus anchors — Fruit of Tizerus flavor-direct, Mindwrack Harpy trivia-canonical-harpy-servitor, Grim Physician trivia-canonical-Returned-from-Erebos-realm, Towering-Wave Mystic flavor-cross-domain-Nerono), [[heliod]] (4 corpus anchors — Glory Bearers flavor-direct sun-crowned-Heliod-named + cult-operative-depicted, Reverent Hoplite cult-operative-procession-to-Heliod-shrine, Return to Nature flavor-direct Heliod's-spear-cast-down via The Cosmogony, Daybreak Chimera trivia-domain-attributed-to-Heliod's-sun-domain). The Heliod-Erebos antagonist-coupling edge per the THB Elspeth-escape arc is now drawable at both ends. Remaining un-split pantheon corpus depth: Athreos (1 god-card via Mystery Booster 146 + Lampad of Death's Vigil THB-103 ferryman-domain attribution), Purphoros (1 via Opaline Unicorn), Pharika (1 via Moss Viper), Karametra (1 via Blessing), Kruphix (1 via Return to Nature), Thassa (1 via Towering Wave Mystic — also at Erebos sub-node). Athreos is now the next-watch deferred split-candidate.
fixes.after: - **Parent-cohort node, not solo-god nodes.** This entry anchors the pantheon as a single character-cohort node, sibling to [[orzhov-syndicate]] / [[azorius-senate]] / [[phyrexia]] / [[aether-foundation]] / [[valgavoth]] / [[izzet-league]] / [[alola-elite-four]]. When an individual god reaches ≥3 corpus depictions across distinct cards, that god gets its own sibling solo character node and this parent node's `appears_on:` for those god-specific cards is mirrored (not delegated — parent retains the appears_on entries; the sub-node duplicates them, per Mogis / Nylea / Iroas precedent). The parent stays as the unifying-faction anchor; solo gods become subsidiaries that inherit institutional context. Current solo sub-nodes commissioned, in canonical tier order — **Major (mono-color principal) gods**: [[heliod]] (white, 4 corpus anchors — Glory Bearers flavor-direct sun-crowned-Heliod-named + cult-operative-depicted, Reverent Hoplite cult-operative-procession-to-Heliod-shrine, Return to Nature flavor-direct Heliod's-spear-cast-down via The Cosmogony, Daybreak Chimera trivia-domain-attributed-to-Heliod's-sun-domain), [[erebos]] (black, 4 corpus anchors — Fruit of Tizerus flavor-direct, Mindwrack Harpy trivia-canonical-harpy-servitor, Grim Physician trivia-canonical-Returned-from-Erebos-realm, Towering-Wave Mystic flavor-cross-domain-Nerono), [[nylea]] (green, 6 corpus anchors); **Minor (two-color) gods**: [[mogis]] (B/R, 6 corpus anchors), [[iroas]] (W/R, 3 corpus anchors). The Heliod-Erebos antagonist-coupling edge per the THB Elspeth-escape arc is now drawable at both ends. Remaining un-split pantheon corpus depth: Athreos (W/B minor, 1 god-card via Mystery Booster 146 + Lampad of Death's Vigil THB-103 ferryman-domain attribution), Purphoros (red major, 1 via Opaline Unicorn), Pharika (B/G minor, 1 via Moss Viper), Karametra (W/G minor, 1 via Blessing), Kruphix (U/G minor, 1 via Return to Nature), Thassa (blue major, 1 via Towering Wave Mystic — also at Erebos sub-node). Athreos is now the next-watch deferred split-candidate.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-162 Caveats parent-cohort sub-node enumeration. Reorder sub-nodes by canonical tier (3 majors first: Heliod, Erebos, Nylea, then 2 minors: Mogis, Iroas) and annotate color-pair attributions on each remaining un-split god so the canonical tiering becomes the node's own structuring metadata.

fixes.before: Theros's red-white god of victory and the bronze-bull twin of Mogis (red-black god of slaughter). Iroas is the pantheon's canonical patron of honorable warfare, athletic competition, and the *just-war* register of conflict — the structural counterpart to Mogis's blood-tithe doctrine within the same fifteen-god roster. He presides over the polis of Akros in northern Phoberos (the Iroas-cult city-state whose annual Iroan Games are the plane's principal athletic competition) and his canonical form is a bronze-armored warrior god with a polished bull-mask face, carrying a great spear and shield. Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Iroas is one of the twelve principal pantheon members; he debuted as a printed card in *Journey into Nyx* (JOU-102, 2014) and has been name-referenced or cult-canonicalized across original *Theros* (2013), *Theros Beyond Death* (2020), and Commander Legends reprint inserts (Armory of Iroas CMR-299).
fixes.after: Theros's red-white god of victory and the bronze-bull twin of Mogis (red-black god of slaughter). Iroas is the pantheon's canonical patron of honorable warfare, athletic competition, and the *just-war* register of conflict — the structural counterpart to Mogis's blood-tithe doctrine within the same fifteen-god roster. He presides over the polis of Akros high in the Katachthon Mountains (the Sparta-coded Iroas-cult city-state whose annual Iroan Games are the plane's principal athletic competition; Phoberos is the minotaur-badlands region geographically west of Akros and south of Skophos, NOT the location of either polis) and his canonical form is a bronze-armored warrior god with a polished bull-mask face, carrying a great spear and shield. Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Iroas is one of the ten two-color minor gods (W/R, the white-red pairing) of the pantheon's 5-major + 10-minor canonical tier-structure; he debuted as a printed card in *Journey into Nyx* (JOU-102, 2014) and has been name-referenced or cult-canonicalized across original *Theros* (2013), *Theros Beyond Death* (2020), and Commander Legends reprint inserts (Armory of Iroas CMR-299).
fixes.rationale: Combined fix for CBG-015 (Akros geography) + CBG-016 (twelve principal). Reframe Iroas's canonical tier per MTG Wiki structure. Note: this edit overlaps with CBG-015's line-31 fix; apply CBG-015 first OR fold this combined version in place of the CBG-015 version.

fixes.before: - **MTG Wiki — Pantheon of Theros** (`https://mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Pantheon_of_Theros`) — parent roster page; situates Iroas as one of the twelve principal pantheon members and confirms the multicolor (red-white) god classification.
fixes.after: - **MTG Wiki — Pantheon of Theros** (`https://mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Pantheon_of_Theros`) — parent roster page; situates Iroas as one of the ten two-color minor gods (white-red pairing) within the canonical 5-major + 10-minor pantheon structure.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-72 canonical-sources citation. Replace the invented 'twelve principal' tier with canonical 'two-color minor'.

fixes.before: Theros's red-black god of slaughter and the dark twin of Iroas (red-white god of victory). Mogis is the pantheon's patron of bloodlust, the canonical lord of Theros's minotaur race, and the named deity of the polis of Skophos in the northern Phoberos canyons — the city-state whose center is the largest temple to Mogis on the plane. Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page) Mogis is one of the twelve principal pantheon members; he debuted as a printed card in *Born of the Gods* (BNG-151, 2014) and has been name-referenced across original *Theros* (2013) and *Theros Beyond Death* (2020) flavor text on cards depicting his minotaur servitors.
fixes.after: Theros's red-black god of slaughter and the dark twin of Iroas (red-white god of victory). Mogis is the pantheon's patron of bloodlust, the canonical lord of Theros's minotaur race, and the named deity of the polis of Skophos (labyrinthine minotaur city carved from red sandstone, located north of the Phoberos badlands — Phoberos itself is the outcast-minotaur-raider region BETWEEN Skophos and Iroas's Akros, not the location of either polis) — the city-state whose center is the largest temple to Mogis on the plane. Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page) Mogis is one of the ten two-color minor gods (B/R, the black-red pairing) of the pantheon's 5-major + 10-minor canonical tier-structure; he debuted as a printed card in *Born of the Gods* (BNG-151, 2014) and has been name-referenced across original *Theros* (2013) and *Theros Beyond Death* (2020) flavor text on cards depicting his minotaur servitors.
fixes.rationale: Combined CBG-015 (Skophos geography) + CBG-016 (twelve principal → ten two-color minor) fix at mogis.md line 35.

fixes.before: - **MTG Wiki — Pantheon of Theros** (`https://mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Pantheon_of_Theros`) — parent roster page; situates Mogis as one of the twelve principal pantheon members and confirms the multicolor (red-black) god classification.
fixes.after: - **MTG Wiki — Pantheon of Theros** (`https://mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Pantheon_of_Theros`) — parent roster page; situates Mogis as one of the ten two-color minor gods (black-red pairing) within the canonical 5-major + 10-minor pantheon structure.

fixes.before: Theros's mono-color white god of the sun, light, law, and justice. Heliod is the pantheon's canonical Greek-mythology homage to Apollo (with overlapping Zeus-pater-of-the-pantheon authority — he is widely depicted across Theros art as the pantheon's nominal first-among-equals though the principal mono-color gods are mechanically equal on devotion-threshold terms) and the patron deity of Akros's sun-cult and the broader Therosian institutions of civic law and hoplite-soldier piety. His signature divine implement is the great golden spear — canonized in *Theros Beyond Death*'s arc as Khrusor and depicted broken on the forest floor in Return to Nature THB-197 — and his canonical Therosian epithet across THB flavor is *Sun-Crowned* (the title under which he was reprinted as Heliod, Sun-Crowned THB-18 in *Theros Beyond Death*). Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Heliod is one of the twelve principal pantheon members and one of the five mono-color principal gods (alongside Erebos, Thassa, Purphoros, Nylea); he debuted as a printed card in original *Theros* (Heliod, God of the Sun, THS-21, 2013) and was reprinted as a re-named-tier *Theros Beyond Death* god-card (Heliod, Sun-Crowned, THB-18, 2020) whose oracle text rewards equipped-weapon-with-deathtouch — the rules-text rendering of the failed-Elspeth-murder turn.
fixes.after: Theros's mono-color white god of the sun, light, law, and justice. Heliod is the pantheon's canonical Greek-mythology homage to Apollo (with overlapping Zeus-pater-of-the-pantheon authority — he is widely depicted across Theros art as the pantheon's nominal first-among-equals though the five mono-color major gods are mechanically equal on devotion-threshold terms) and the patron deity of Akros's sun-cult and the broader Therosian institutions of civic law and hoplite-soldier piety. His signature divine implement is the great golden spear — canonized in *Theros Beyond Death*'s arc as Khrusor and depicted broken on the forest floor in Return to Nature THB-197 — and his canonical Therosian epithet across THB flavor is *Sun-Crowned* (the title under which he was reprinted as Heliod, Sun-Crowned THB-18 in *Theros Beyond Death*). Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Heliod is one of the five mono-color major gods of the pantheon (alongside Erebos black, Thassa blue, Purphoros red, Nylea green; the canonical structure is 5 majors + 10 two-color minors = 15 total); he debuted as a printed card in original *Theros* (Heliod, God of the Sun, THS-21, 2013) and was reprinted as a re-named-tier *Theros Beyond Death* god-card (Heliod, Sun-Crowned, THB-18, 2020) whose oracle text rewards equipped-weapon-with-deathtouch — the rules-text rendering of the failed-Elspeth-murder turn.
fixes.rationale: Correct line-34 heliod.md lead-paragraph. Replace 'twelve principal pantheon members AND one of the five mono-color principal gods' (the redundant invented-tier framing) with the canonical 'five mono-color major gods' tier-structure with explicit 5+10=15 enumeration.

fixes.before: Theros's mono-color black god of the dead and lord of the Theros Underworld. Erebos is the pantheon's canonical Greek-mythology homage to Hades — warden of mortal souls after death, presiding deity of the five-river Underworld geography (Tartyx, Tizerus, Nerono, Phylias and the unnamed river-of-forgetting) from his palace at the Underworld's deepest reach. Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Erebos is one of the twelve principal pantheon members and one of the five mono-color principal gods (alongside Heliod, Thassa, Purphoros, Nylea); he debuted as a printed card in original *Theros* (Erebos, God of the Dead, THS-86, 2013) and was reprinted as a re-named-tier *Theros Beyond Death* god-card (Erebos, Bleak-Hearted, THB-84, 2020).
fixes.after: Theros's mono-color black god of the dead and lord of the Theros Underworld. Erebos is the pantheon's canonical Greek-mythology homage to Hades — warden of mortal souls after death, presiding deity of the five-river Underworld geography (Tartyx, Tizerus, Nerono, Phylias and the unnamed river-of-forgetting) from his palace at the Underworld's deepest reach. Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Erebos is one of the five mono-color major gods of the pantheon (alongside Heliod white, Thassa blue, Purphoros red, Nylea green; the canonical structure is 5 majors + 10 two-color minors = 15 total); he debuted as a printed card in original *Theros* (Erebos, God of the Dead, THS-86, 2013) and was reprinted as a re-named-tier *Theros Beyond Death* god-card (Erebos, Bleak-Hearted, THB-84, 2020).
fixes.rationale: Correct line-35 erebos.md lead-paragraph. Replace 'twelve principal pantheon members AND one of the five mono-color principal gods' with the canonical 5-major + 10-minor structure.

fixes.before: Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Nylea is one of the twelve principal pantheon members and one of the five mono-color principal gods (alongside Heliod, Erebos, Thassa, Purphoros);
fixes.after: Per the *Theros Pantheon* roster (MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page), Nylea is one of the five mono-color major gods of the pantheon (alongside Heliod white, Erebos black, Thassa blue, Purphoros red; the canonical structure is 5 majors + 10 two-color minors = 15 total);
fixes.rationale: Correct nylea.md line 35 lead-paragraph fragment. Replace 'twelve principal + five mono-color principal' redundancy with the canonical 5-major + 10-minor framing.

fixes.before: - **MTG Wiki — Pantheon of Theros** (`https://mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Pantheon_of_Theros`) — parent roster page; situates Heliod as one of the five mono-color principal pantheon members and confirms the white god-of-the-sun classification.
fixes.after: - **MTG Wiki — Pantheon of Theros** (`https://mtg.fandom.com/wiki/Pantheon_of_Theros`) — parent roster page; situates Heliod as one of the five mono-color major gods within the canonical 5-major + 10-minor pantheon structure and confirms the white god-of-the-sun classification.
fixes.rationale: Correct heliod.md line-77 canonical-sources citation. The existing phrasing is less wrong than the others (it says 'five mono-color principal' which IS canonical) but adopting the unified phrasing across all sub-nodes is cleaner.

verification_command: cd 'C:\Users\alexa\Desktop\Bulk Graph Bundler' && grep -c 'twelve principal\|twelve Nyx-born' cards/_characters/iroas.md cards/_characters/mogis.md cards/_characters/heliod.md cards/_characters/erebos.md cards/_characters/nylea.md cards/_characters/theros-pantheon.md
uncertainty_notes: (1) MTG Wiki Pantheon of Theros page was verified via WebSearch (the page 403s on direct WebFetch); the canonical structure of '5 major mono-color gods + 10 two-color minor gods = 15 total' is corroborated by multiple secondary sources (CBR, Draftsim, Jay Annelli, World Anvil) in the WebSearch results. (2) The 'twelve principal' framing may be a corpus-internal shorthand the author intended for 'the canonical-Olympian-tier subset of the pantheon' — i.e. the 12 gods minus Xenagos/Ephara/Keranos who are demigod-ascended or post-BNG additions. If this was the intent, the framing is still misleading because (a) it isn't the MTG-Wiki-canonical tier-split, (b) Ephara and Keranos are full minor gods per canon (BNG printings, two-color, identical god-card mechanic to the other minors), and (c) Xenagos is ALSO a full god post-ascension per Wizards canon. The 5+10 tier-split is the only Wizards-canonical tiering. (3) The combined CBG-015 + CBG-016 fix at iroas.md line 31 and mogis.md line 35 (and similar combinations elsewhere) requires careful application order — if both sidecars are applied separately by independent passes, the second pass will fail to find its old_string. Recommended: apply CBG-016 OR the combined diff at any double-affected line, not both separately.
